metadata.title: Planet Labs & the commercial space-imaging / Earth-observation industry — operators, the NRO/NGA demand side, launch, and the 2025-26 consolidation (documented, dated)
metadata.status: Company facts, constellations, contracts (as competed IDIQ ceilings vs guaranteed revenue — distinguished), M&A, IPOs, and government awards are individually sourced FACT (SEC/NRO/NGA/company releases + trade press). Fleet 'operational' counts and a few aggregator financials are FLAGGED where secondary. Forward guidance and unconfirmed classified figures are graded weak.
metadata.frame: The commercial Earth-observation (EO) layer feeding US/allied intelligence and the AI-analytics economy. Planet Labs (NYSE: PL) runs the largest daily-scan fleet (Dove/SuperDove) plus tasking (SkySat->Pelican) and hyperspectral (Tanager). The competitive field churned hard in 2025-26: Maxar rebranded to Vantor (intelligence) + Lanteris (space) (Oct 1 2025, Advent-owned); Capella Space was acquired by IonQ (Jul 15 2025); HawkEye 360 (NYSE: HAWK), SpaceX (Nasdaq: SPCX) and Firefly (Nasdaq: FLY) went public; Spire sold its AIS business to Kpler; Albedo exited commercial imagery. Demand is bifurcated: the NRO buys the pixels (EOCL: Maxar/Vantor ~$3.24B, BlackSky ~$1.02B, Planet ~$146M initial) and the NGA does analytics/distribution (Luno A $290M + Luno B $200M IDIQs; GEGD portal; Ukraine surge). Attaches to NRO, NGA, SDA, US_Government, IonQ, Tether, SpaceX/Starshield, and the geopolitics-space-layer / defense threads.

planet_labs: Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL; co-founders Will Marshall/CEO + Robbie Schingler; gov work via Planet Labs Federal, Inc.). Constellations: PlanetScope (Dove/SuperDove) daily ~3m 8-band (continuously replenished — 36 SuperDoves launched Jan 14 2025 + 36 more Nov 28 2025); SkySat (~50cm tasking, ex-Google Terra Bella 2017; being succeeded); Pelican (SkySat successor, ~50cm Gen-1 to ~30cm Gen-2, ~8 on orbit by May 2026); Tanager hyperspectral/methane (with Carbon Mapper; Tanager-1 launched Aug 16 2024, 11,000+ methane plumes; SWIR-only build deal Apr 30 2026); and 'Owl' (announced Oct 7 2025, near-daily 1m, onboard NVIDIA GPUs, demo late 2026). AI/'data+software' shift: Anthropic/Claude partnership (Mar 6 2025), NVIDIA 'GPU-native AI engine' (Mar 16 2026; ran inference in space on a Pelican Apr 7 2026), Planet Insights Platform (on the 2023 Sinergise/Sentinel Hub buy). Financials FY2026 (ended Jan 31 2026): revenue $307.7M (+26%), FIRST full-year positive Adj EBITDA +$15.5M, GAAP net loss $(246.9)M of which ~$161.4M is a NON-CASH warrant revaluation (not operations), cash+ST investments $640.1M, backlog $900.4M (+79%, 98% recurring). grade: fact (FY26 figures via the 8-K; SkySat operational count and FY27 guidance FLAGGED as secondary).
planet_contracts_ownership: The premise of 'one big NGA check' is wrong — Planet's biggest deals are commercial/allied-sovereign: SKY Perfect JSAT (Japan) $230M (Jan 29 2025, dedicated ~10-sat Pelican capacity — commercial, not JAXA); Germany EUR240M/~$280M (Jul 1 2025, defense/security); Sweden low-nine-figure sovereign military (Jan 12 2026, first sovereign sat flew May 3 2026). US intel: NRO EOCL 10-yr (May 2022, via Planet Labs Federal, initial value ~$146M — an initial award, NOT a 10-yr ceiling comparable to Maxar/BlackSky); NGA Luno B slot (Jan 2025) with 8-figure task orders (e.g., $12.8M AAMOR maritime-AI Oct 16 2025 with SynMax); a June 2025 defense cluster (DIU/DoD Hybrid Space Architecture/INDOPACOM, US Navy, NATO). Ownership: Alphabet/Google is the largest single holder (~10-11%, legacy Terra Bella) — FLAGGED (verify % vs latest 13F/proxy). grade: fact (contracts/dates); ownership % flagged.
the_landscape: The 2025-26 field (with corrections): (1) MAXAR -> VANTOR (intelligence) + LANTERIS (space), rebranded Oct 1 2025, both owned by Advent International (2023 ~$6.4B take-private); WorldView Legion (6 sats, 30cm, up to 15 revisits/day); the biggest NRO EOCL (~$3.24B ceiling) + runs NGA's GEGD portal + primary Ukraine imagery; new AI products Raptor (GPS-denied nav) + Tensorglobe. (2) BlackSky (NYSE: BKSY) — Gen-3 35cm on Rocket Lab Electron (schedule slipped: 3 of 6 planned 2025 launches flew), Spectra AI; NRO EOCL ~$1.02B; NGA Luno A/B; Indonesia ~$50M. (3) Satellogic (Nasdaq: SATL) — Kargieman/CEO; ~18-19 NewSats (NOT the '50+' marketing); Tether's $30M is CONVERTIBLE DEBT (as-converted ~15-17%, CFIUS-pending) — Mnuchin's Liberty Strategic is the largest equity holder (27.39%, Mnuchin chairman; Gen. Dunford director). (4) Capella Space (SAR) ACQUIRED BY IonQ (closed Jul 15 2025, ~$311-443M all-stock) — first US commercial SAR to work with NRO (2019). (5) Umbra (SAR, private) — 25cm routine; NRO SCE Stage III; SpaceWERX STRATFI up to $60M. (6) ICEYE (Finland, SAR) — largest commercial SAR (62 launched cumulatively); Series F >EUR1B at ~$12B valuation (Jun 9 2026); increasingly SELLS sovereign systems (Germany/Rheinmetall JV ~$2B Dec 2025; Poland/Portugal/Greece); heavy Ukraine role. (7) Spire Global (NYSE: SPIR) — sold AIS/maritime to Kpler ($233.5M, Apr 2025); now GNSS-RO weather/aviation. (8) HawkEye 360 (NYSE: HAWK) — IPO'd May 7 2026 (~$416M raised, ~$2.4B); largest commercial RF-geolocation constellation; NRO anchor customer; FY2025 first full-year profit. (9) Albedo — EXITED commercial imagery ~Oct 2025 to build VLEO buses (Clarity-1 validated 10cm then reentered Jun 10 2026). (10) Airbus Defence & Space (Euronext: AIR) — Pleiades Neo (only 2 of 4; Neo 5/6 lost in the Vega-C VV22 failure Dec 2022); 'Project Bromo' MoU (Oct 23 2025) to merge Airbus/Leonardo/Thales space arms (target 2027, EC decision not before H2 2027). (11) Synspective (Japan, SAR) — public (Tokyo 290A since Dec 2024); Japan MoD constellation slot. (12) China: ChangGuang/Jilin-1 (state-linked; world's largest sub-meter commercial EO; OFAC-sanctioned Dec 12 2023 for Wagner imagery, new Iran-related OFAC sanction May 8 2026; an Apr 17 2025 State Dept item was a WARNING not a sanction) and Spacety/Changsha Tianyi (OFAC-sanctioned Jan 26 2023 for supplying SAR of Ukraine to a Wagner-linked firm). grade: fact (each with source); fleet 'operational' vs 'launched' counts flagged.
the_gov_demand: Division of labor: the NRO buys the pixels; the NGA does analytics/distribution. NRO EOCL (Electro-Optical Commercial Layer, 10-yr 2022-32): Maxar/Vantor up to ~$3.24B, BlackSky up to ~$1.02B, Planet initial ~$146M. NRO Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) two-stage BAA by phenomenology: SAR survivors Capella/ICEYE US/Umbra (to Jan 2027); RF (HawkEye 360 et al.); hyperspectral (BlackSky, Orbital Sidekick, Pixxel, Planet, et al.); EO (Airbus US, Albedo, Hydrosat, Muon, Turion); new CSO approach (2026). NGA Luno: Luno A 5-yr IDIQ $290M ceiling (Sept 13 2024, 10 vendors incl. Airbus US, Booz Allen, BlackSky, Maxar, Ursa Space); Luno B $200M ceiling (Jan 15 2025, 13 vendors, adds BAE, Deloitte, Planet Labs Federal) — combined ~$490M; task orders 8-figure. Ukraine/COCOMs: NGA more than doubled commercial-EO buys, conduit is the GEGD portal (prime Maxar/Vantor, 400k+ users); Ukraine access was suspended Mar 7 2025 then restored ~Mar 18 2025 (policy-driven). Proliferated LEO: SDA's PWSA (Tranche 1 first launch Sept 10 2025); NRO's own proliferated recon uses SpaceX Starshield (with Northrop). grade: fact (NRO/NGA releases); the '$1.8B 2021 Starshield' figure is Reuters-reported (flagged).
the_launch_and_consolidation: Launch layer: SpaceX (Nasdaq: SPCX, IPO'd Jun 12 2026) Falcon 9 + Transporter rideshare is the EO-smallsat workhorse, and Starshield builds the NRO proliferated recon constellation; Rocket Lab (Nasdaq: RKLB) Electron is the dedicated small-EO/SAR launcher (Synspective/BlackSky/Capella/iQPS), moving up-stack (Neutron slipped to Q4 2026; $816M SDA prime Dec 2025; bought Geost EO/IR sensors; intends its own constellation); ULA (Boeing-Lockheed JV) Vulcan NSSL-certified but HALTED after a Feb 12 2026 SRB anomaly (CEO Bruno left for Blue Origin Dec 2025); Blue Origin New Glenn (booster landing Nov 2025) then a pad explosion destroyed LC-36 May 28 2026; Firefly (Nasdaq: FLY, IPO'd Aug 7 2025; Blue Ghost lunar landing Mar 2025); Sierra Space ($550M Series C at $8B, Mar 2026). Consolidation themes: RF SIGINT coalescing around HawkEye 360 (absorbed Aurora Insight/Maxar RF + ISA; Kleos bankrupt 2023); SAR bifurcating (Capella->IonQ; ICEYE selling sovereign systems); European imaging/manufacturing heading toward the Airbus-Leonardo-Thales 'Bromo' entity; commercial RO-weather a NOAA three-supplier market (Spire/PlanetiQ/GeoOptics). grade: fact; two launchers (Vulcan, New Glenn) are impaired as of mid-2026 (fluid).
the_honest_reading: Commercial space-imaging is now a strategic ISR layer: a handful of operators (Vantor/ex-Maxar, Planet, BlackSky for EO; Capella/ICEYE/Umbra for SAR; HawkEye 360 for RF) sell into a bifurcated US demand structure — the NRO buys imagery under EOCL/SCE, the NGA buys analytics under Luno and distributes via GEGD — with the Ukraine war (and the Mar 2025 suspend-then-restore episode) showing how load-bearing and policy-contingent the commercial layer has become. Planet's specific niche is DAILY global coverage + a fast AI/analytics pivot (Anthropic, NVIDIA, 'Owl'/onboard GPUs), now at its first full year of positive Adjusted EBITDA. Care is warranted on three recurring traps the sources themselves flag: (1) IDIQ 'ceilings' are competed maxima, not guaranteed revenue (Maxar's $3.24B EOCL is not comparable to Planet's ~$146M initial award); (2) 'launched' satellite counts overstate 'operational' fleets (ICEYE 62 launched, Jilin-1 117 claimed vs ~63 trackable); (3) headline GAAP losses can be dominated by non-cash items (Planet's warrant revaluation; Satellogic's Tether-note fair-value swings). Ownership/control threads worth tracking for the wider graph: IonQ->Capella (quantum + SAR), Tether + Mnuchin's Liberty Strategic -> Satellogic, Advent -> Vantor/Lanteris, and Google's ~10% of Planet. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
